name: Kumari Worship
description: Living goddess tradition where a pre-pubescent Newar girl from the Shakya clan is selected as the incarnation of Taleju (Durga). The Royal Kumari of Kathmandu resides in Kumari Ghar at Durbar Square.
associated_materials: Ceremonial palanquin, red dress, special eye makeup (fire eye), golden crown

name: Newari Metalwork
description: Traditional lost-wax casting (cire perdue) and repoussé metalwork practiced by Newar artisans for centuries. Produces bronze statues, temple ornaments, and ritual objects.
associated_materials: Copper, bronze, gold, beeswax, clay molds, chisels, hammers

name: Thangka Painting
description: Buddhist scroll painting tradition depicting mandalas, deities, and religious scenes. Paubha is the Newar variant. Requires years of apprenticeship and strict iconographic rules.
associated_materials: Mineral pigments, gold leaf, cotton canvas, bamboo frame, natural glue

name: Deusi Bhailo
description: Traditional singing and dancing performed during Tihar (festival of lights). Groups go door to door performing Deusi (by boys) and Bhailo (by girls) songs in exchange for gifts and money.
associated_materials: Madal drum, jhyali cymbals, traditional costumes

name: Dhime Dance
description: Traditional Newar dance performed to the beat of the Dhime drum. An integral part of Newar festivals and processions, especially during Indra Jatra and other jatras.
associated_materials: Dhime drum, bhusyah cymbals, traditional white and red costumes

name: Charya Nritya
description: Sacred Buddhist tantric dance performed by Vajracharya priests. Depicts various Buddhist deities through mudras and movements. UNESCO Intangible Heritage candidate.
associated_materials: Ritual crown, silk robes, sacred masks, hand bells (ghanta)

name: Jyapu Farming Traditions
description: Agricultural practices of the Jyapu (Newar farmer) caste. Includes unique rice cultivation techniques, seasonal rituals, and the famous Jyapu dance during festivals.
associated_materials: Traditional wooden plough (hali), sickle, rice seedlings, ceremonial pots

name: Lakhe Dance
description: Masked demon dance performed during Indra Jatra and other festivals. The dancer wears a fearsome red mask and dances wildly to drum beats, chasing children through the streets.
associated_materials: Red demon mask, hairy costume, large earrings, ankle bells, Dhime drum
